creator: bdrewery@FreeBSD.org
text:
Why make this change?

This goes against our plans to have all ports depend only on ports. I
admit this has not been communicated well. libexecinfo should probably
be moved to /usr/lib/private on head to prevent ports from using it.

On a side note, this change would be better as a Mk/Uses/execinfo.mk and
having a USES=execinfo to not have all the duplicated logic. Similar to
readline.mk. However, we are actively working to move libreadline into
/usr/lib/private as well and then ensure ports are using the port version.

Also, many of these ports (before the change) appear to already support
the base version. Since it is LIB_DEPENDS= libexecinfo.so, if the
library is not found in the ld path, it will pull in devel/libexecinfo.
On 10+ it will find it in the path and NOT pull in devel/libexecinfo. So
the check for OSVERSION is not needed.

-- 
Regards,
Bryan Drewery
